title: Discovery Journeys
summary: Typical discovery journeys start from home or search, pass through playlists or radio, and often dead-end when users cannot escape their taste profile or find unfamiliar artists without curated playlist dependency.

evidence.label: Hard to find new artists or genres
evidence.support_line: 62 reviews across Reddit and Social Media

evidence.label: Discover Weekly / Release Radar feel stale
evidence.support_line: 38 reviews across Social Media, Reddit and Play Store

evidence.label: Recommendations feel irrelevant or off-target
evidence.support_line: 15 reviews across Reddit and Social Media

key_pain_points: Hard to find new artists or genres
Discover Weekly / Release Radar feel stale
root_causes: Weak exploration tools: There are not enough surfaces, controls, or settings to actively go discover something new
Playlist dependency: Listeners can only discover through Discover Weekly / Release Radar / Daily Mix and have no alternatives
UI / navigation friction: Layout, search, or settings make it slow or confusing to act on a listening intent
affected_segments: Discovery Seeker: Hard to find new artists or genres
Playlist User: Discover Weekly / curated playlists feel stale
Heavy Listener: Discover Weekly / curated playlists feel stale
Casual Listener: Ads disrupt the discovery flow
Premium User: Discover Weekly / curated playlists feel stale
Free User: Ads disrupt the discovery flow
unmet_needs: Need dedicated discovery surfaces beyond Discover Weekly
Need better personalization that learns from feedback
Need fresher and more accurate recommendations
product_focus_areas: Onboarding paths into first meaningful discovery win
Seamless transitions from search to sustained exploration
recommended_actions: Map end-to-end discovery journeys and remove dead-end paths in search and home.
Add journey-aware prompts at high-intent moments (search, save, skip).
Measure time-to-first-new-artist as a core discovery KPI.
